data.content:
751	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
752	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
753	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
754	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
755	  ⟨GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (IdentityComponent G) (identityComponentSubgroupFunctor G)
756	    (identityComponentRepresentableBy G)⟩
757	
758	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
759	
760	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
761	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
762	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
763	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
764	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
765	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
766	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
767	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
768	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
769	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.
770	
771	iter-189 partial progress: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` conjunct closes
772	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The
773	remaining two conjuncts (`QuasiCompact`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`)
774	require the group-structure argument and are bundled into the residual
775	sorry. -/
776	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
777	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
778	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
779	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
780	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
781	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
782	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
783	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
784	  · -- `LocallyOfFiniteType`: `(IdentityComponent G).hom` unfolds to
785	    -- `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`. Open immersion ∘ LFT
786	    -- is LFT (`locallyOfFiniteType_of_isOpenImmersion` +
787	    -- `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`).
788	    change LocallyOfFiniteType ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
789	    infer_instance
790	  · -- `QuasiCompact ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible`: Kleiman's group-theoretic
791	    -- argument. `α(U × U) = G^0` quasi-compact via the affine `U × U`;
792	    -- geometric irreducibility via base change to `\bar k`, picking a
793	    -- smooth affine `U ⊆ G^0`, and translating `hg⁻¹U` to cover. Cannot
794	    -- close axiom-clean without the substrate `isSubgroupHomomorphism`
795	    -- + EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 / 4.6.1 / EGA I 6.1.10 (not yet in Mathlib).
796	    sorry
797	
798	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
799	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
800	
801	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
802	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
803	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
804	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
805	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
806	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
807	
808	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
809	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
810	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
811	identifying the two iterated constructions.
812	
813	CLOSED (run 0005, session 0011): the `_grpInst` slot via
814	`CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`, the `_locFTInst` slot via
815	Mathlib's base-change stability of `LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the iso slot
816	(Stacks 04KS / EGA IV₂ 4.5.16) by the carrier identification
817	`fst⁻¹(G⁰-carrier) = (G_K)⁰-carrier` inside `|G ×ₖ Spec K|`:
818	
819	- `⊆` (connectedness): `fst⁻¹(carrier)` is the image of the open immersion
820	  `pullback ι fst`, whose source is connected because
821	  `pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ` is connected — Mathlib's
822	  connected-pullback instance fed by
823	  `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` and universal openness of
824	  morphisms to the spectrum of a field — and it contains the identity
825	  point of `G_K` (which lies over `e` by the base-change compatibility of
826	  the unit of `grpObjMkPullbackSnd`);
827	- `⊇` (clopen): `fst⁻¹(carrier)` is clopen and contains the identity point
828	  of `G_K`, hence contains its connected component.
829	
830	The isomorphism is `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` composed with the
831	pasting isomorphism `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`. -/
832	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
833	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
834	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
835	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
836	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
837	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
838	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
839	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
840	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
841	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
842	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by
843	  intro φ G_K
844	  -- `GrpObj G_K` via the category-theoretic base-change lemma
845	  -- `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`. The instance bridge
846	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`
847	  -- when `G` is an over-category object whose right component is the unique
848	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`). `letI` (not `haveI`) so the group
849	  -- structure stays definitionally transparent for the carrier computation.
850	  letI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
851	  letI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
852	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the
853	  -- base change of `G.hom` along `φ`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable
854	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).
855	  haveI hGK_lft : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom :=
856	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))
857	  refine ⟨⟨hGK_grp, hGK_lft, ?_⟩⟩
858	  -- §1: connectivity substrate.
859	  letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G
860	  haveI : IsIntegral (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) := inferInstance
861	  haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
862	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))
863	  haveI : Subsingleton ↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).left :=
864	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))
865	  haveI : UniversallyOpen (IdentityComponent G).hom := inferInstance
866	  haveI : ConnectedSpace ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) := connectedSpace_spec_of_isDomain K
867	  haveI hP : ConnectedSpace ↥(pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := inferInstance
868	  haveI hP' : ConnectedSpace ↥(pullback ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom) φ) := hP
869	  haveI hQ : ConnectedSpace
870	      ↥(pullback (identityComponentCarrier G).ι (pullback.fst G.hom φ)) :=
871	    ((pullbackRightPullbackFstIso G.hom φ
872	      (identityComponentCarrier G).ι).hom.homeomorph.connectedSpace_iff).mpr hP'
873	  -- §2: the identity point of `G_K` lies over the identity point of `G`
874	  -- (base-change compatibility of the unit of `grpObjMkPullbackSnd`).
875	  have hεfst : (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (Over.pullback φ)).left ≫
876	      pullback.fst (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).hom φ = φ := by
877	    have hcond : pullback.fst (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).hom φ =
878	        pullback.snd (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).hom φ ≫ φ := by
879	      simpa using pullback.condition
880	        (f := (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))).hom) (g := φ)
881	    rw [hcond, CategoryTheory.Over.ε_pullback_left, ← Category.assoc]
882	    erw [IsIso.inv_hom_id]
883	    rw [Category.id_comp]
884	  have hfst : ⇑(pullback.fst G.hom φ) (identitySectionPoint G_K) =
885	      identitySectionPoint G := by
886	    have h1 := congrArg
887	      (fun m : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K))) ⟶ G_K =>
888	        ⇑m.left.base (default : ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K))))
889	      (CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd_one (f := G.hom) (g := φ))
890	    calc ⇑(pullback.fst G.hom φ) (identitySectionPoint G_K)

897	              (default : ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K)))) :=

data.content:
=== theorem/def/lemma headers between 832 and 995 ===
theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
=== end of baseChangeIso region: lines 970-996 ===

Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
`prp:pic0`). -/

namespace Scheme

/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.

Defined as the identity component
`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
base field.

iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
  sorry

424:  -- `f default = identitySectionPoint G` (by definition of
425:  -- `identitySectionPoint`); the carrier underlying set is
426:  -- `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`.
434:  @IsOpenImmersion.lift (identityComponentCarrier G).toScheme (Spec (.of k))
435:    G.left (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
451:  -- `(IdentityComponent G).hom = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`
453:  change identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = _

WT=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge

input.description: Find the two helper defs used by isSubgroupHomomorphism

data.content:
687:private noncomputable def identityComponentSubgroupFunctor :
720:private noncomputable def identityComponentRepresentableBy :

680	        (identityComponentInclusion G ≫ GrpObj.inv (X := G)) := by
681	      conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc, identityComponentFactor_comp G f hf]
682	      exact Hom.inv_def f
683	    rw [hfac]
684	    exact (range_comp_left_subset _ _).trans (range_inclusion_inv_subset G)
685	
686	/-- The presheaf of groups `T ↦ {f : T ⟶ G | im f ⊆ G⁰}`. -/
687	private noncomputable def identityComponentSubgroupFunctor :
688	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
689	  obj T := GrpCat.of (identityComponentSubgroup G T.unop)
690	  map {T T'} φ := GrpCat.ofHom
691	    { toFun := fun f => ⟨φ.unop ≫ f.1, (range_comp_left_subset _ _).trans f.2⟩
692	      map_one' := Subtype.ext (by
693	        show φ.unop ≫ (1 : T.unop ⟶ G) = (1 : T'.unop ⟶ G)
694	        simp only [Hom.one_def]
695	        rw [← Category.assoc, comp_toUnit])
696	      map_mul' := fun f g => Subtype.ext (by
697	        show φ.unop ≫ (f.1 * g.1) = (φ.unop ≫ f.1) * (φ.unop ≫ g.1)
698	        simp only [Hom.mul_def]
699	        rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]) }
700	  map_id T := by
701	    ext f
702	    exact Category.id_comp _
703	  map_comp {T T' T''} φ ψ := by
704	    ext f
705	    exact Category.assoc _ _ _
706	
707	/-- The natural bijection `(T ⟶ G⁰) ≃ {f : T ⟶ G | im f ⊆ G⁰}`. -/
708	private noncomputable def identityComponentHomEquiv (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) :
709	    (T ⟶ IdentityComponent G) ≃ ↥(identityComponentSubgroup G T) where
710	  toFun u := ⟨u ≫ identityComponentInclusion G,
711	    (range_comp_left_subset _ _).trans (range_inclusion_left_subset G)⟩
712	  invFun f := identityComponentFactor G f.1 f.2
713	  left_inv u := by
714	    apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
715	    exact (IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
716	      (u ≫ identityComponentInclusion G).left _ u.left rfl).symm
717	  right_inv f := Subtype.ext (identityComponentFactor_comp G f.1 f.2)
718	
719	/-- `IdentityComponent G` represents the subgroup presheaf. -/
720	private noncomputable def identityComponentRepresentableBy :
721	    (identityComponentSubgroupFunctor G ⋙ forget GrpCat).RepresentableBy
722	      (IdentityComponent G) where
723	  homEquiv {T} := identityComponentHomEquiv G T
724	  homEquiv_comp {T T'} f g := by
725	    apply Subtype.ext
726	    show (f ≫ g) ≫ identityComponentInclusion G = f ≫ (g ≫ identityComponentInclusion G)
727	    exact Category.assoc _ _ _
728	
729	end SubgroupStructure
730	
731	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
732	
733	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
734	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
735	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
736	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
737	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
738	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
739	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
740	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
741	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
742	
743	CLOSED (run 0005, session 0011): via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` applied to
744	the subgroup presheaf `identityComponentSubgroupFunctor` — the group
745	structure on `Hom(T, G⁰)` is the subgroup of `Hom(T, G)` of morphisms
746	landing in the carrier, with closure under `mul`/`inv` provided by the
747	connectedness core lemmas `range_tensor_mul_subset` /
748	`range_inclusion_inv_subset` above. The induced group law on `G⁰` is by
749	construction compatible with the inclusion (the Yoneda equivalence is
750	`u ↦ u ≫ identityComponentInclusion G`). -/
751	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]

data.content:
=== head ===
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib

/-!
# Connected + rational section ⟹ geometrically connected (Stacks 04KV / 037Q)

This file proves the descent substrate needed by `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`:
a connected `k`-scheme `X` admitting a `k`-rational section `s : Spec k ⟶ X` of its
structural morphism `f : X ⟶ Spec k` is **geometrically connected** (Stacks 04KV,
EGA IV₂ 4.5.14). This unblocks the `AJC.picrep` identity-component cone
(`identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` and its consumers).

## Architecture

* **§A Algebra (Stacks 05P3-flavoured).** Over an algebraically closed field `k`,
  the tensor product `A ⊗[k] B` of two field extensions is a domain.
  - `exists_algHom_ker_eq`: maximal ideals of finite-type `k`-algebras have residue
    field `k` (Zariski's lemma + `IsAlgClosed`), packaged as evaluation `k`-algebra
    homomorphisms.
  - `isDomain_tensorProduct_of_finiteType`: for `R` a finite-type `k`-domain and `A/k`
    a field extension, `R ⊗[k] A` is a domain. The classical argument: coordinates of
    a zero-divisor pair multiply into every maximal ideal (evaluate via
    `exists_algHom_ker_eq` and use linear independence of a `k`-basis of `A`), hence
    into the Jacobson radical `= 0` (`R` is a Jacobson domain).
  - `Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain_of_isAlgClosed`: the general case by exhausting
    the first factor by finite-type subalgebras (flatness over a field gives
    injectivity of `R ⊗[k] B ⟶ A ⊗[k] B`).

* **§B Geometry.**
  - `connectedSpace_pullback_of_isAlgClosed` (Stacks 0363): for `k` **algebraically
    closed** and `X/k` connected, `X ×ₖ Spec L` is connected for every extension
    `L/k`. Proof: the projection `X ×ₖ Spec L ⟶ X` is geometrically connected (its
    field-valued fibers are `Spec (K ⊗[k] L)`, connected by §A) and open (`Spec L ⟶
    Spec k` is universally open, being a morphism to the spectrum of a field), so
    Mathlib's `GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace` applies.
  - `connectedSpace_pullback_algebraicClosure` (Stacks 04KV, algebraic step): for `X/k`
    connected with a rational section, `X ×ₖ Spec k̄` is connected. The projection
    `p : X ×ₖ Spec k̄ ⟶ X` is open, closed (integral) and surjective, and the fiber
    over the section point `x₀` is a single point (`κ(x₀) = k`); a nontrivial clopen
    partition upstairs would give two clopen sets downstairs both equal to `X`,
    contradicting the singleton fiber at `x₀`.
  - `geometricallyConnected_of_connectedSpace_of_section` (Stacks 04KV): assembly.
    For arbitrary `L/k`, embed `k̄ ↪ L̄ := (AlgebraicClosure L)` over `k`, apply the
    two previous steps to get `X ×ₖ Spec L̄` connected, and descend along the
    surjection `X ×ₖ Spec L̄ ⟶ X ×ₖ Spec L`.

## References

- Stacks: 04KV (section ⟹ geometrically connected), 0363 (separably closed base),
  05P3 (tensor product of extensions over algebraically closed field is a domain),
  0383 (schemes over a field are universally open over it).
=== declaration headers ===
78:theorem exists_algHom_ker_eq (R : Type*) [CommRing R] [Algebra k R]
101:theorem isDomain_tensorProduct_of_finiteType
172:theorem _root_.Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain_of_isAlgClosed
232:lemma connectedSpace_spec_of_isDomain (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] :
242:theorem geometricallyConnected_pullback_fst_of_isAlgClosed [IsAlgClosed k]
264:theorem connectedSpace_pullback_of_isAlgClosed [IsAlgClosed k]
280:theorem connectedSpace_pullback_algebraicClosure
377:theorem geometricallyConnected_of_connectedSpace_of_section

data.content:
=== identityComponent_geometricallyConnected def + proof ===
476:this lemma explicitly via `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G`.
488:chain `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G` with

---
this lemma explicitly via `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G`.

Derived from:
- `identityComponent_connectedSpace`: `ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left`
  (axiom-clean iter-192).
- `identityComponentSection_isSection`: existence of a section
  `Spec k ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left` (axiom-clean iter-193).
- `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`: Stacks 04KU helper
  (axiom-clean since run 0005 session 0007 via
  `Picard/GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean`).

Downstream consumers can
chain `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G` with
`UniversallyOpen` of `Spec k → Spec k` (which holds via
`[IsIntegral Y] [Subsingleton Y] ⟹ UniversallyOpen f`) to derive
`ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom g)` for any
`g : Y ⟶ Spec (.of k)` from a `ConnectedSpace Y`. -/
private theorem identityComponent_geometricallyConnected
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
  geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
    (IdentityComponent G).hom (identityComponentSection G)
    (identityComponentSection_isSection G)

 ```` tag to the body, which is the only signal agents read to decide           
 whether to keep or close the item. ``--to`` addresses the item to a recipient  
 (e.g. ``--to project:foo`` to message another project).                        
                                                                                
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *  --body                TEXT                      Inbox item body.          │
│                                                    [required]                │
│    --kind                [hint|issue|protection|i  Item kind.                │
│                          nfo|memory]               [default: hint]           │
│    --project             TEXT                      Scope the item to a       │
│                                                    project (what it is       │
│                                                    ABOUT).                   │
│    --to                  TEXT                      Recipient the item is     │
│                                                    FOR: horizon | ground |   │
│                                                    human | project:<name>.   │
│    --author              TEXT                      Who is writing it (human  │
│                                                    / horizon / ground).      │
│    --agent               TEXT                      Finer-grained author      │
│                                                    identity (e.g. a subagent │
│                                                    descriptor name like      │
│                                                    'blueprint-reviewer');    │
│                                                    recorded in metadata so   │
│                                                    the author stays the      │
│                                                    conventional role.        │
│    --persistent                                    Tag a standing hint the   │
│                                                    agent never closes.       │
│    --temporary                                     Tag a one-shot hint the   │
│                                                    agent closes once used.   │
│    --pending                                       Mark not-ready (withheld  │
│                                                    from the agents) instead  │
│                                                    of agent-ready.           │
│    --json                                          Emit machine-readable     │
│                                                    JSON to stdout.           │
│    --help        -h                                Show this message and     │
│                                                    exit.                     │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground --agent diff-auditor --project Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge --body "Diff audit run 0005 T5 (commit 6e7f7ae): PASS, no defects.

Verified:
- IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism + IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso genuinely closed. Kernel #print axioms = [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound] for both — no sorryAx. Helper chain (identityComponentSubgroupFunctor/RepresentableBy, identityComponent_geometricallyConnected via geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section) is sorry-free.
- No buried sorry/admit/native_decide/new axiom anywhere in the +1103 added lines (all textual 'sorry' hits are in docstrings: 'sorry-free', 'typed-sorry').
- IdentityComponent.lean now has exactly 6 genuine code sorries (796 isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible; 995/1036/1089/1107/1132 Pic0Scheme-family), all Pic0/EGA-blocked, matching the 8->6 report claim (note: git parent 6e7f7ae~1 showed 9 bare-sorry lines, so net was 9->6 via the +546/-195 refactor; current=6 is correct and the two named theorems are the closures).
- New file GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean (437 lines): sorry-free, builds green, main theorem geometricallyConnected_of_connectedSpace_of_section axiom-clean. Content matches stated purpose (Stacks 04KV/0363/05P3 connected+section => geometrically connected).
- Blueprint tex \leanok anchors point only at the new sorry-free decls + the two closures; none attached to still-sorried isFiniteType/Pic0 decls. Blueprint reconciliation is expected Ground rewiring.

Hygiene note (pre-existing, NOT this run): stray scratch files RationalCurveIso.{new,skeletal,body} persist in project root; they live in baseline commit 180b985 (mtime Jun 17) and are already tracked in memory (albanese-stray-blueprint-fragments)." 2>&1 | tail -5

text:
Audit complete. Filed inbox item I-0053. Summary of findings:

**Commit `6e7f7ae` (run 0005, T5) on Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge — PASS, no defects.**

- **Two theorems genuinely closed, axiom-clean.** `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` (term-mode via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` on the subgroup presheaf) and `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` (carrier identification `fst⁻¹(G⁰) = (G_K)⁰` via `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`). Kernel `#print axioms` on both returns exactly `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — no `sorryAx`. Their transitive helper chain (`identityComponentSubgroupFunctor`/`RepresentableBy`, `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` → `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`) is sorry-free. Reasoning is sound; the Yoneda-subgroup-presheaf and clopen-carrier approaches are correctly implemented and the module builds green.

- **No buried sorry/admit/native_decide/axiom** in the +1103 added lines. All textual `sorry` matches are in docstrings ("sorry-free", "typed-sorry").

- **Sorry count:** `IdentityComponent.lean` now has exactly **6** genuine code sorries (line 796 `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`; 995/1036/1089/1107/1132 in the `Pic0Scheme` family), all EGA/Pic0-blocked — matches the report's "→6". Minor bookkeeping note: the git parent `6e7f7ae~1` had 9 bare-sorry lines (report/memory said 8), so the net was 9→6 through the large +546/−195 refactor. Not a defect.

- **New file `GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean`** (437 lines): sorry-free, builds green, main theorem `geometricallyConnected_of_connectedSpace_of_section` axiom-clean. Content matches its stated purpose (Stacks 04KV/0363/05P3, connected + rational section ⟹ geometrically connected).

- **Blueprint tex:** `\leanok` anchors point only at the new sorry-free declarations and the two closures — none attached to still-sorried `isFiniteType`/`Pic0` decls. Expected Ground reconciliation.

- **Hygiene (pre-existing, not this run):** stray scratch files at `/AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/RationalCurveIso.{new,skeletal,body}` persist in the project root. They live in baseline commit `180b985` (mtime Jun 17), are not part of this commit's 3-file diff, and are already documented in memory (`albanese-stray-blueprint-fragments`). Not attributable to this session.

Relevant files:
- `/AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`
- `/AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean`
